name: Santo Christo
description_short: Santo Christo Federal Credit Union is a Massachusetts-based credit union serving 2,111 members with $17.94M in assets and community-focused financial services.
description_long:
Santo Christo Federal Credit Union was established in 1954 and has operated for 72 years as a federally-chartered credit union headquartered in Fall River, Massachusetts. The institution serves a defined community membership base and maintains a single branch location at 149 Columbia Street in Fall River, with approximately $17.94 million in total assets as of March 2025. The credit union employs 5 full and part-time staff members and is led by CEO/Manager Margaret A. Diogo and Chairperson Gregory Brilhante.

The credit union offers standard consumer banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/Debit Card programs, and a loan portfolio comprised of real estate loans ($7M), unsecured loans ($1.2M), and other loan products ($3M). They provide international remittances and low-cost wire transfer services. Their balance sheet reflects $11.27 million in total loans against $16.31 million in member deposits, indicating an active lending operation focused on the community they serve.

Santo Christo's distinguishing characteristics include its Federal Credit Union designation (Charter 9822), FHLB membership status, and community-based membership model as opposed to employer-sponsored or low-income-focused designations. The institution maintains NCUSIF deposit insurance protection and underwent audit in March 2025. With only one branch location and modest asset size compared to regional banking competitors, Santo Christo functions as a hyperlocal financial cooperative.

As a small community credit union, Santo Christo offers limited branch accessibility and product sophistication relative to larger financial institutions. However, for members within their defined community, they provide personalized service and competitive member-owned cooperative benefits. The organization demonstrates financial stability with positive net income of $135,828 and manageable loan loss provisions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of deposit or share certificates
pros: Federal credit union with NCUSIF insurance protection for member deposits
FHLB member providing access to wholesale funding and liquidity resources
Offers international remittance and low-cost wire transfer services
Diverse loan portfolio including real estate, unsecured, and other loan products
Provides ATM/Debit card programs and basic digital banking access
Recently audited financial institution (March 2025) with transparent reporting
cons: Single branch location limits accessibility for members outside Fall River area
Small asset base ($17.9M) limits product innovation and service breadth
Limited staff (5 employees) may restrict service hours and customer support availability
No mention of online banking, mobile app, or digital-first services
Membership restricted to community members rather than open enrollment
best_for: Fall River, MA residents seeking community-based credit union membership
Small business owners and individuals needing personalized loan services
Members making international remittances or requiring wire transfer capabilities
Savers prioritizing federally-insured deposits with cooperative ownership benefits
diagnosis: Santo Christo Federal Credit Union is best for Fall River-area residents seeking a stable, community-focused credit union with personalized service and NCUSIF-insured deposits. The main caveat is the single physical branch location and limited digital banking infrastructure, making this institution less suitable for members requiring extensive branch networks or sophisticated online services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
